skills.name: get_current_time
skills.description: Get the current date and time in the user's timezone. Answers questions like: what time is it, what is today's date, current time, current date, tell me the time, Wie spät ist es, aktuelle Uhrzeit, welches Datum haben wir, Uhrzeit jetzt, Datum und Uhrzeit, quelle heure est-il, date et heure actuelle, che ora è, data e ora corrente

parameters.name: format
parameters.description: The format for the date/time output

skills.name: get_system_info
skills.description: Get information about the Klacks system, version, and environment

skills.name: get_user_context
skills.description: Get basic information about the current user (name, ID, tenant, current page, timezone). Use get_user_permissions instead if you need to check what actions the user is allowed to perform.

skills.name: validate_calendar_rule
skills.description: Validates a calendar/holiday rule and calculates the resulting date. Supports fixed dates (MM/DD), Easter-relative (EASTER+XX) and SubRules (SA+2;SU+1)

skills.name: navigate_to
skills.description: Navigate to any page in Klacks the current user is allowed to see. Pages: dashboard, client-list (employee overview), new-employee (create employee form), edit-employee (edit existing employee, needs entityId), schedule, absences, client-availability, settings, group-list, new-group (create group form), edit-group (edit existing group, needs entityId), group-structure, shift-list, new-shift (create shift template), edit-shift (edit shift template, needs entityId), cut-shift (split or trim a shift, needs entityId), container-template (edit shift container template, needs entityId), inbox, messaging, profile, floor-plan, period-closing, klacksy-training (admin training review). Additional pages may be added by feature plugins. For finding a person by name use search_and_navigate instead.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: The page to navigate to. client-list for employee overview; new-employee for create employee form; edit-employee for edit existing employee (needs entityId); new-group for create group form; edit-group for edit existing group (needs entityId); new-shift for create shift template; edit-shift for edit shift template (needs entityId); cut-shift for split or trim a shift (needs entityId); container-template for edit shift container template (needs entityId); klacksy-training for admin training review.

parameters.name: entityId
parameters.description: The ID of the entity to view (required for edit-employee, edit-group, edit-shift, cut-shift, container-template)

parameters.name: tab
parameters.description: The specific tab to open on the page

parameters.name: target
parameters.description: Optional sub-target ID on the destination page. Must match a data-klacksy-target attribute.

skills.name: get_page_controls
skills.description: Returns the UI controls for a given page. Use page="list" to get all available page keys. Use a specific page key (e.g. "settings-owner-address") to get the controls for that page, including selectors, control types, labels, and routes. This helps you navigate and interact with the UI.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: The page key to retrieve controls for. Use "list" to see all available pages.

skills.name: add_ai_memory
skills.description: Adds a new persistent memory entry for the AI assistant. Memories persist across all conversations and help the assistant remember important facts, user preferences, and system knowledge. Embeddings are generated automatically for semantic search.

parameters.name: key
parameters.description: Short identifier or title for the memory entry.

parameters.name: content
parameters.description: The actual memory content to remember.

parameters.name: isPinned
parameters.description: If true, this memory is always included in the context.

skills.name: add_client_to_group
skills.description: Adds a client/employee to a group. The group membership can have optional validity dates. Use this when you need to assign a person to a team, department, or organizational unit.

parameters.name: clientId
parameters.description: The unique ID (GUID) of the client to add to the group

parameters.name: groupId
parameters.description: The unique ID (GUID) of the group

parameters.name: validFrom
parameters.description: Start date of group membership (format: YYYY-MM-DD). Defaults to today.

parameters.name: validUntil
parameters.description: End date of group membership (format: YYYY-MM-DD). Leave empty for indefinite.

skills.name: assign_contract_to_client
skills.description: Assigns an existing contract to a client/employee. This creates a client-contract association with validity dates. Only one contract can be active at a time per client. Use this to set up employment contracts.

parameters.name: clientId
parameters.description: The unique ID (GUID) of the client

parameters.name: contractId
parameters.description: The unique ID (GUID) of the contract to assign

parameters.name: fromDate
parameters.description: Start date of the contract assignment (format: YYYY-MM-DD)

parameters.name: untilDate
parameters.description: End date of the contract assignment (format: YYYY-MM-DD). Leave empty for indefinite.

parameters.name: setAsActive
parameters.description: Whether to set this as the active contract (will deactivate other contracts)

skills.name: configure_heartbeat
skills.description: Configure the proactive heartbeat monitoring. Use 'enable' to activate monitoring, 'disable' to stop it, 'configure' to set checklist and interval, or 'status' to view current configuration.

parameters.name: checklist
parameters.description: JSON array of items to monitor, e.g. ["shift conflicts", "contract expirations"]

parameters.name: intervalMinutes
parameters.description: How often to check in minutes (default: 30)

skills.name: create_branch
skills.description: Creates a new branch (Filiale) in the system. A branch represents a physical office location with name, address, phone, and email.

skills.name: create_contract
skills.description: DEPRECATED — do not use. To assign a contract to an employee use assign_contract_by_name (by name) or assign_contract_to_client (by GUID). Contracts are read from the contract table; they are not created from a hardcoded type mapping.

parameters.name: employeeId
parameters.description: ID of the employee to create the contract for

parameters.name: canton
parameters.description: Swiss canton code for the contract

parameters.name: startDate
parameters.description: Contract start date in format YYYY-MM-DD

parameters.name: endDate
parameters.description: Contract end date in format YYYY-MM-DD (optional for unlimited contracts)

skills.name: create_employee
skills.description: Creates a new client (employee, external employee or customer) with personal data, address, email and phone. Always also creates a mandatory membership starting on memberSince — ALWAYS ask the user when the employee starts working (Eintrittsdatum / start date / ab wann), and resolve answers like "heute"/"sofort"/"today" to today's date in YYYY-MM-DD before calling. Set entityType to choose Employee/ExternEmp/Customer. To assign a contract use assign_contract_by_name; to add to a group use add_client_to_group (only with REAL contracts/groups from list_contracts/list_groups).

parameters.name: memberSince
parameters.description: Date when the employee starts working (Eintrittsdatum / Anstellungsbeginn / start date). Format YYYY-MM-DD. REQUIRED — always ask the user explicitly. Resolve casual answers like "heute"/"sofort"/"today" to today's date before sending. Drives Membership.ValidFrom and therefore the schedule's plannability boundary for this employee.

parameters.name: state
parameters.description: State or region abbreviation (e.g. BE, ZH). Auto-detected from ZIP code if not provided.

parameters.name: country
parameters.description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code in upper case (e.g. CH for Switzerland/Schweiz, DE for Germany/Deutschland, FR, IT, AT). Convert any country name the user gives into its ISO code. Defaults to CH when omitted.

parameters.name: email
parameters.description: Email address of the client. Strongly recommended; without an email there is no email-based planning.

parameters.name: phone
parameters.description: Phone number of the client. Strongly recommended.

parameters.name: entityType
parameters.description: Type of client: Employee (own employee), ExternEmp (external employee) or Customer. Defaults to Employee.

parameters.name: proceedWithoutContact
parameters.description: Set to true ONLY after the user has explicitly declined to provide address, email or phone. By default address, email and phone must be collected before the client is created; if any is missing the call is rejected so you can ask for it.

skills.name: create_user
skills.description: Creates a new system user account (login) in the user administration. This is for authentication accounts, not HR employees. Requires admin permissions. A username is auto-generated from first and last name. A random password is generated and returned.

skills.name: delete_ai_memory
skills.description: Deletes a persistent memory entry by its ID. Use get_ai_memories first to find the memory ID you want to delete.

parameters.name: memoryId
parameters.description: The ID of the memory entry to delete.

skills.name: delete_branch
skills.description: Deletes a branch (Filiale) by its ID. Use list_branches first to find the branch ID. Requires admin permissions.

parameters.name: branchId
parameters.description: The ID of the branch to delete

skills.name: delete_system_user
skills.description: Deletes a system user account permanently by user ID. Use list_system_users first to find the user ID. Requires admin permissions.

parameters.name: userId
parameters.description: The ID of the user to delete

skills.name: get_ai_guidelines
skills.description: Retrieves the AI assistant's active guidelines. Use this when the user asks about current rules, or to check guidelines before updating them with update_ai_guidelines. Guidelines define rules for behavior and function usage.

skills.name: get_ai_memories
skills.description: Retrieves the AI assistant's persistent memories. Use this when the user asks 'What do you know about X?' or 'What memories do you have?'. Supports three search modes: semantic search (searchQuery uses vector similarity), text search (searchTerm uses keyword matching), or category filter.

parameters.name: searchQuery
parameters.description: Semantic search query â€” finds memories with similar meaning (uses embeddings).

parameters.name: searchTerm
parameters.description: Exact text search â€” finds memories containing this keyword.

skills.name: get_ai_soul
skills.description: Retrieves the AI assistant's personality definition (soul sections). Use this when the user asks about the assistant's personality, identity, or behavior rules, or when you need to check current settings before updating them. Sections: identity, personality, tone, boundaries, communication_style, values, domain_expertise, error_handling.

parameters.name: sectionType
parameters.description: Optional: Filter to retrieve a specific soul section.

skills.name: get_client_details
skills.description: Retrieves complete details for a client/employee including their contracts, group memberships, addresses, and contact information. Use this to check what contracts a person has, which groups they belong to, or to get their full profile.

parameters.name: clientId
parameters.description: The unique ID (GUID) of the client to retrieve

skills.name: get_general_settings
skills.description: Retrieves the general application settings including the application name. Use this to check what the application is called or to verify settings before updating them.

skills.name: get_user_permissions
skills.description: Returns the current user's role and detailed permission list. Use this BEFORE attempting operations that require specific permissions to verify the user has access. For basic user info (name, ID, tenant) use get_user_context instead.

skills.name: list_branches
skills.description: Lists all branches (Filialen) in the system. Returns branch IDs, names, addresses, phone numbers, and emails.

parameters.name: searchTerm
parameters.description: Optional search term to filter branches by name

skills.name: list_contracts
skills.description: Lists all available contracts in the system. Optionally filter by canton via CalendarSelection. Returns contract details including name, hours, and rates. Use this to find contract IDs before assigning contracts to clients.

parameters.name: activeOnly
parameters.description: If true, only return contracts that are currently valid (ValidFrom <= today and ValidUntil is null or >= today)

parameters.name: canton
parameters.description: Optional canton/state abbreviation (e.g. 'BE', 'ZH', 'AG') to filter contracts by their CalendarSelection. Use lookup_location to determine the canton first.

skills.name: list_groups
skills.description: Lists all available groups (teams, departments, organizational units) in the system. Optionally filter by canton via CalendarSelection. Returns group names and IDs. Use this to find group IDs before adding clients to groups.

parameters.name: searchTerm
parameters.description: Optional search term to filter groups by name

parameters.name: rootOnly
parameters.description: If true, only return root-level groups (no parent)

parameters.name: canton
parameters.description: Optional canton/state abbreviation (e.g. 'BE', 'ZH', 'AG') to filter groups by their CalendarSelection. Use lookup_location to determine the canton first.

skills.name: lookup_location
skills.description: Looks up a Swiss location by city name or postal code. Returns the city, canton/state abbreviation, country and associated zip codes. Use this in guided workflows to determine the canton before filtering contracts and groups.

parameters.name: city
parameters.description: City name to look up (e.g. 'Bern', 'Zürich', 'Aarau'). Case-insensitive.

parameters.name: zip
parameters.description: Postal code to look up (e.g. 3011, 8000). Either city or zip must be provided.

skills.name: list_llm_models
skills.description: Lists all configured LLM models with their provider, status, costs, and capabilities.

parameters.name: searchTerm
parameters.description: Optional search term to filter models by name

skills.name: list_llm_providers
skills.description: Lists all configured LLM providers with their status, priority, and whether an API key is configured.

skills.name: list_scheduling_rules
skills.description: Lists all scheduling rules (e.g. maximum shift duration, minimum rest time, overtime limits) with their configured values and thresholds.

parameters.name: searchTerm
parameters.description: Optional search term to filter scheduling rules by name

skills.name: list_system_users
skills.description: Lists all system user accounts (login accounts) in the user administration. Returns user IDs, usernames, names, emails, and roles. Use this to find user IDs for further operations like deletion or role changes.

parameters.name: searchTerm
parameters.description: Optional search term to filter users by name or email

skills.name: search_in_list
skills.description: Open the entity list page (clients, groups, shifts) with the visible search filter pre-filled, so the user sees the matching rows directly in the list. Use this when the user wants to BROWSE or REVIEW matches in the workplace list (e.g. 'such mir Gasparoli', 'zeig mir die Maiers in der Liste'). For directly opening a single record use search_and_navigate. For criteria-based DB queries returning a chat list use search_employees.

parameters.name: entityType
parameters.description: Which workplace list to open and filter

parameters.name: searchQuery
parameters.description: Search term to pre-fill into the list's search filter

skills.name: open_schedule
skills.description: Open the schedule (Dienstplan / Einsatzplan / shift planner) page, optionally pre-filtered to a specific group. Pass groupName whenever the user just edited a person/employee that was added to a group — the schedule should open with that group's filter active so the user sees the planning for the right team. If the group cannot be resolved, the skill opens the schedule without a group filter rather than failing.

parameters.name: groupName
parameters.description: Optional name of the planning group to pre-select (e.g. 'Bern', 'Köniz', 'Thun'). If omitted, the schedule opens with the user's currently selected group (or none). When the conversation just touched a client/employee that is assigned to a group, default to that group's name.

skills.name: search_and_navigate
skills.description: Search for an entity (employee, customer, group, shift) by name and navigate directly to it. Use this when the user wants to open or edit a specific person or entity (e.g., 'Open customer Max MÃ¼ller', 'Show me Heribert Gasparoli'). If multiple matches are found, all will be shown.

parameters.name: entityType
parameters.description: Type of entity to search for

parameters.name: searchQuery
parameters.description: Name or search term to find the entity

parameters.name: action
parameters.description: Action to take after finding: view or edit

skills.name: search_employees
skills.description: Search for employees or customers by criteria (name, canton, type) and return a list of matches. Use this for filtered searches and lists. To search AND directly navigate to a specific person, use search_and_navigate instead.

parameters.name: searchTerm
parameters.description: Search term for name, company, or other text fields

parameters.name: entityType
parameters.description: Filter by entity type: Employee, ExternEmp, or Customer

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of results to return

skills.name: set_user_group_scope
skills.description: Sets the group visibility scope for a user. This controls which groups a user can see in the system. Provide the user ID and one or more group names to assign. Existing group scope entries for the user are replaced.

parameters.name: userId
parameters.description: The ID of the user to set the group scope for

parameters.name: groupNames
parameters.description: Comma-separated list of group names to assign (e.g. 'Deutschweiz Mitte, Romandie')

skills.name: test_imap_connection
skills.description: Tests the IMAP connection using the currently saved IMAP settings. The password must already be entered in the settings UI. Returns success, error details, and message count.

skills.name: test_smtp_connection
skills.description: Tests the SMTP connection using the currently saved email settings. The password must already be entered in the settings UI. Returns success or detailed error message for troubleshooting.

skills.name: update_ai_guidelines
skills.description: Replaces the AI assistant's guidelines with new content. WARNING: This deactivates ALL existing guidelines and creates a new one. Guidelines define rules for behavior, function usage, and permission handling.

parameters.name: guidelines
parameters.description: The complete guidelines text defining the AI's behavioral rules.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: A short name for this guidelines set (e.g. 'Default Guidelines').

skills.name: update_ai_memory
skills.description: Updates an existing persistent memory entry. You can update the key, content, category, importance, or pinned status. If content changes, the embedding is regenerated automatically.

parameters.name: memoryId
parameters.description: The ID of the memory entry to update.

parameters.name: key
parameters.description: New short identifier or title for the memory entry.

parameters.name: category
parameters.description: New category for the memory entry.

parameters.name: isPinned
parameters.description: Whether this memory should always be included in context.

skills.name: update_ai_soul
skills.description: Updates a specific section of the AI assistant's personality definition (soul). Sections: identity, personality, tone, boundaries, communication_style, values, domain_expertise, error_handling. Each section can be independently updated. Changes are versioned with full audit trail.

parameters.name: content
parameters.description: The content for this soul section.

parameters.name: sortOrder
parameters.description: Display order (0 = first). Default: auto-assigned based on section type.

skills.name: update_spam_filter_settings
skills.description: Updates the spam filter configuration. Set spam/uncertain thresholds (0.0 to 1.0, higher = stricter) and enable/disable LLM-based email classification. All parameters are optional â€” only provided values will be updated.

parameters.name: spamThreshold
parameters.description: Spam score threshold 0.0â€“1.0 above which messages are classified as spam

skills.name: update_web_search_settings
skills.description: Updates the web search provider configuration. Set the search provider, API key, and maximum results per query. All parameters are optional â€” only provided values will be updated.

parameters.name: apiKey
parameters.description: API key for the web search provider

parameters.name: maxResults
parameters.description: Maximum number of search results to return per query

skills.name: validate_address
skills.description: Validates an address and returns geocoding information (coordinates, formatted address). Detects the state/region from the ZIP code. Use this before creating an employee to verify the address exists.

skills.name: web_search
skills.description: Searches the internet for current, real-time information. Use this for email provider settings (SMTP/IMAP servers, ports, SSL), current events, or information that requires up-to-date data. Do NOT use for general knowledge questions you can answer from training data.

parameters.name: maxResults
parameters.description: Maximum number of results to return (default: 5)

skills.name: update_general_settings
skills.description: Updates general application settings. Currently supports changing the application name. The application name is displayed in the browser title bar and the application header.

parameters.name: appName
parameters.description: The new application name to set. This is displayed in the browser title and application header.

skills.name: update_owner_address
skills.description: Updates the owner/company address in settings. IMPORTANT: state and country are REQUIRED and must always be provided.

parameters.name: state
parameters.description: State or canton abbreviation (e.g. BE, ZH)

skills.name: update_email_settings
skills.description: Updates the outgoing email (SMTP) settings. All parameters are optional - only provided values will be updated.

skills.name: update_imap_settings
skills.description: Updates the incoming email (IMAP) settings. All parameters are optional - only provided values will be updated.

skills.name: update_work_settings
skills.description: Updates work-related settings. All parameters are optional - only provided values will be updated. Includes vacation days, probation/notice periods, payment interval, surcharge rates, and visibility ranges.

parameters.name: vacationDaysPerYear
parameters.description: Number of vacation days per year

skills.name: update_scheduling_defaults
skills.description: Updates scheduling default settings. All parameters are optional - only provided values will be updated.

parameters.name: schedulingMaxWorkDays
parameters.description: Maximum consecutive work days per scheduling period

parameters.name: schedulingMinRestDays
parameters.description: Minimum rest days between work periods

parameters.name: schedulingMaxOptimalGap
parameters.description: Maximum optimal gap between shifts in hours

parameters.name: schedulingMaxDailyHours
parameters.description: Maximum daily working hours for scheduling

parameters.name: schedulingMaxWeeklyHours
parameters.description: Maximum weekly working hours for scheduling

skills.name: update_deepl_settings
skills.description: Updates the DeepL translation API key. The API key is stored encrypted in settings.

parameters.name: apiKey
parameters.description: The DeepL API key for translation services

skills.name: create_llm_provider
skills.description: Creates a new LLM provider configuration. The provider will be visible in Settings > LLM Providers.

parameters.name: providerId
parameters.description: Unique provider identifier (e.g., openai, anthropic, deepseek)

parameters.name: baseUrl
parameters.description: Base URL for the provider API

parameters.name: isEnabled
parameters.description: Whether provider is enabled (true/false), defaults to true

skills.name: update_llm_provider
skills.description: Updates an existing LLM provider configuration. Only provided values will be changed.

parameters.name: baseUrl
parameters.description: Base URL for the provider API

skills.name: delete_llm_provider
skills.description: Deletes an LLM provider configuration.

skills.name: create_llm_model
skills.description: Creates a new LLM model configuration. The model will be available in the chat model selector.

parameters.name: providerId
parameters.description: Provider ID this model belongs to

parameters.name: isDefault
parameters.description: Set as default model (true/false, default false)

skills.name: update_llm_model
skills.description: Updates an existing LLM model configuration. Only provided values will be changed.

parameters.name: providerId
parameters.description: Provider ID this model belongs to

skills.name: delete_llm_model
skills.description: Deletes an LLM model configuration.

skills.name: create_scheduling_rule
skills.description: Creates a new scheduling rule with work time limits and surcharge rates.

skills.name: update_scheduling_rule
skills.description: Updates an existing scheduling rule. Only provided values will be changed.

parameters.name: ruleId
parameters.description: The scheduling rule ID to update

skills.name: delete_scheduling_rule
skills.description: Deletes a scheduling rule.

parameters.name: ruleId
parameters.description: The scheduling rule ID to delete

skills.name: update_branch
skills.description: Updates an existing branch/location. Only provided values will be changed.

skills.name: get_email_settings
skills.description: Reads the current outgoing email (SMTP) settings. Returns server, port, timeout, SSL, authentication type, disposition notification, read receipt, reply-to, mark and SMTP username. Does NOT return the password.

skills.name: get_imap_settings
skills.description: Reads the current incoming email (IMAP) settings. Returns server, port, SSL, folder, poll interval and username. Does NOT return the password.

skills.name: get_owner_address
skills.description: Reads the current owner/company address from the settings. Returns all address fields: name, supplement, street, zip, city, state, country, phone, email.

skills.name: get_work_settings
skills.description: Retrieves employment and payroll settings: vacation days per year, probation period, notice period, payment interval, surcharge rates (night, holiday, saturday, sunday), and day visibility ranges. For working hour limits, overtime thresholds, and scheduling rule parameters use get_scheduling_defaults instead.

skills.name: get_scheduling_defaults
skills.description: Retrieves scheduling parameters: working hour limits, overtime thresholds, guaranteed/maximum/minimum hours, full-time hours, and scheduling rule limits (max work days, min rest days, pause hours, max consecutive days, max daily/weekly hours). For surcharge rates (night, holiday, weekend) or employment terms (vacation, probation, notice period) use get_work_settings instead.

skills.name: get_deepl_settings
skills.description: Retrieves the DeepL translation API settings. Returns whether an API key is configured (not the key itself for security).

skills.name: get_spam_filter_settings
skills.description: Retrieves spam filter configuration including spam threshold, uncertain threshold, and whether LLM-based filtering is enabled.

skills.name: get_web_search_settings
skills.description: Retrieves web search configuration including provider, max results, and whether an API key is configured.

skills.name: list_agent_skills
skills.description: Lists all registered skills with their name, description, execution type, and status. Use this for self-reflection — to understand what capabilities are available.

parameters.name: searchTerm
parameters.description: Optional search term to filter skills by name or description

skills.name: create_agent_skill
skills.description: Creates a new UiAction skill at runtime. The new skill will be immediately available. Use this to learn new UI shortcuts or automation patterns that the user teaches you.

parameters.name: description
parameters.description: LLM-facing description that explains when and how to use this skill

parameters.name: triggerKeywords
parameters.description: Comma-separated keywords that trigger this skill

parameters.name: synonyms
parameters.description: JSON object mapping language codes to synonym arrays for keyword matching, e.g. {"de":["wort1","wort2"],"en":["word1","word2"]}

skills.name: update_agent_skill
skills.description: Updates an existing skill's description, parameters, or configuration. Use this to improve skill descriptions for better selection accuracy, or to adjust handler steps, trigger keywords, or synonyms.

parameters.name: skillName
parameters.description: Name of the skill to update

parameters.name: handlerSteps
parameters.description: New handler steps as JSON array

parameters.name: synonyms
parameters.description: JSON object mapping language codes to synonym arrays for keyword matching, e.g. {"de":["wort1","wort2"],"en":["word1","word2"]}

skills.name: delete_agent_skill
skills.description: Disables a skill by name. The skill will no longer be available for use. Only user-created skills can be deleted, not system skills.

parameters.name: skillName
parameters.description: Name of the skill to disable

skills.name: review_skill_suggestions
skills.description: Lists auto-detected skill gaps and pending suggestions. Allows the admin to accept a suggestion (which creates the skill) or dismiss it. Actions: 'list' shows all pending, 'accept' creates the skill, 'dismiss' removes the suggestion.

parameters.name: gapId
parameters.description: ID of the skill gap record to accept or dismiss (required for accept and dismiss actions)

skills.name: list_absence_types
skills.description: Lists all configured absence types (e.g. Vacation, Sick Leave, Unpaid Leave) with their settings like color, default duration, and weekend/holiday rules. Use this to understand which absence categories exist before searching for specific client absences.

parameters.name: language
parameters.description: Language code for absence names (de, en, fr, it)

parameters.name: includeHidden
parameters.description: Include absence types that are hidden in the Gantt chart

skills.name: search_client_absences
skills.description: Search for client/employee absences within a time period. Returns which employees are absent when, filtered by name, absence type, year, or date range. Use list_absence_types first if you need to know which absence types exist.

parameters.name: searchTerm
parameters.description: Client or employee name to search for

parameters.name: year
parameters.description: Year to search absences in (default: current year)

parameters.name: startDate
parameters.description: Start date for date range filter (yyyy-MM-dd). Overrides year parameter.

parameters.name: endDate
parameters.description: End date for date range filter (yyyy-MM-dd). Overrides year parameter.

parameters.name: absenceType
parameters.description: Filter by absence type name (e.g. 'Urlaub', 'Vacation', 'Krank'). Matches against all languages and abbreviations.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of clients to return (default: 10, max: 50)

skills.name: create_shift
skills.description: Creates a new shift (Schicht/Dienst/Auftrag) in the scheduling system. The shift is automatically sealed and made available for planning. For a 24/7 operation split into 3 shifts, call this 3 times with different time ranges (e.g. 07:00-15:00, 15:00-23:00, 23:00-07:00). Use list_groups first to find group IDs.

parameters.name: abbreviation
parameters.description: Short abbreviation (e.g. 'FD', 'SD', 'ND'). Auto-generated from name if not provided.

parameters.name: endTime
parameters.description: End time in HH:mm format (e.g. '15:00', '23:00', '07:00'). Can be before startTime for overnight shifts.

parameters.name: fromDate
parameters.description: Start date of the shift validity in YYYY-MM-DD format. Defaults to today.

parameters.name: untilDate
parameters.description: End date of the shift validity in YYYY-MM-DD format. Leave empty for unlimited/ongoing shifts.

parameters.name: weekdays
parameters.description: Which days the shift is active. Use the keyword 'all' (every day), 'weekdays' (Mon-Fri) or 'weekend' (Sat-Sun); or a comma-separated list of canonical English day tokens: Mon,Tue,Wed,Thu,Fri,Sat,Sun. Map any language the user uses to these canonical tokens.

parameters.name: sumEmployees
parameters.description: Number of employees needed per shift occurrence

parameters.name: groupIds
parameters.description: Comma-separated list of group IDs (GUIDs) to assign the shift to. Use list_groups to find IDs.

skills.name: create_test_environment
skills.description: Creates a complete test environment for autofill testing in ONE call. Creates multiple employees with realistic Swiss addresses, assigns them to a group, creates a shared contract, and optionally creates 24/7 shifts (Fruhdienst/Spaetdienst/Nachtdienst). Use this when the user asks for 'Testmitarbeiter', 'Testdaten', or 'Testumgebung'.

parameters.name: city
parameters.description: City for employee addresses (e.g. 'Bern'). Default: Bern

parameters.name: numberOfEmployees
parameters.description: Number of test employees to create (max 10). Default: 5

parameters.name: groupName
parameters.description: Name of the group to assign employees to (e.g. 'Bern'). Default: same as city

parameters.name: canton
parameters.description: Swiss canton code for the contract (e.g. 'BE'). Default: BE

parameters.name: guaranteedHours
parameters.description: Guaranteed monthly hours for the contract. Default: 172.2

parameters.name: shiftStartDate
parameters.description: Start date for shifts in YYYY-MM-DD format. Default: today

parameters.name: createShifts
parameters.description: Whether to create 3 shifts (Fruhdienst 07-15, Spaetdienst 15-23, Nachtdienst 23-07). Default: true

parameters.name: sumEmployees
parameters.description: Number of employees needed per shift. Default: 1

skills.name: start_autowizard
skills.description: Runs the AutoWizard chain (Wizard 1 Planner + Wizard 2 Harmonizer + Wizard 3 Holistic Harmonizer) sequentially to auto-fill a schedule for a group and period. Mirrors what the 'Autofill-Assistent' button in the schedule header triggers: takes group and period, resolves visible agents and shifts automatically, kicks off the orchestrator job, returns the jobId. Final completion arrives via the AutoWizard SignalR hub (event OnCompleted). Use this when the user says: 'plane mir den Plan', 'autofill', 'autowizard', 'erzeuge Vorschlag für Mai', 'plan the schedule', 'run wizards'. The chain produces three analyse_scenarios (Plan, Harmonizer, final 'Auto-Erstellung'); the user can later accept_scenario or reject_scenario.

parameters.name: groupId
parameters.description: UUID of the group to plan for (e.g. the schedule view's selectedGroup like Bern).

parameters.name: periodFrom
parameters.description: Period start date in ISO format YYYY-MM-DD (e.g. 2026-05-01).

parameters.name: periodUntil
parameters.description: Period end date in ISO format YYYY-MM-DD, inclusive (e.g. 2026-05-31).

parameters.name: agentIds
parameters.description: Optional comma-separated list of client UUIDs. If omitted, all clients with active membership in the group are used.

parameters.name: shiftIds
parameters.description: Optional comma-separated list of shift UUIDs. If omitted, shifts visible for the group hierarchy in the period are used.

parameters.name: analyseToken
parameters.description: Optional source-scenario token to chain from. Null = run on the main schedule.

parameters.name: language
parameters.description: Optional UI language for the Wizard 3 LLM stage (e.g. 'de', 'en'). Falls back to engine default.

skills.name: place_work
skills.description: Assigns a client (agent) to a shift on a specific date — creates a single Work entry in the main schedule or in a named analyse scenario. Uses the existing BulkAddWorks pipeline so period-hour recalculation and validation logic stays consistent. Use when the user says: 'plane Yasmine am 15.05. auf Frühschicht', 'add Coline to N10 on May 18', 'assign agent to shift'.

parameters.name: clientId
parameters.description: UUID of the client/agent to schedule.

parameters.name: shiftId
parameters.description: UUID of the shift to assign.

parameters.name: startTime
parameters.description: Optional HH:mm; defaults to the shift's start.

parameters.name: endTime
parameters.description: Optional HH:mm; defaults to the shift's end.

parameters.name: workTime
parameters.description: Optional hours value; defaults to end-start (wrapping past midnight).

parameters.name: information
parameters.description: Free-text note for this work entry.

parameters.name: analyseToken
parameters.description: Optional scenario UUID; null = main schedule.

skills.name: delete_work
skills.description: Soft-deletes a single Work entry (one assignment of a client to a shift on a date). Period-hour recalc runs automatically. Use when the user says: 'lösche diese Schicht', 'remove work', 'cancel assignment'. Cannot delete works that are sealed/locked.

skills.name: accept_scenario
skills.description: Accepts an AnalyseScenario — promotes its works/breaks to the main schedule. Use after start_autowizard finishes and the user wants to commit the wizard output, or after any other scenario producer (Wizard 1/2/3 individually, manual scenarios). The scenario's analyse_token becomes null on its works (they merge into the main schedule).

skills.name: confirm_work
skills.description: Locks a Work entry at LockLevel=Confirmed (1) so Wizards 1+2+3 will no longer modify the cell. Use when the user says: 'fix this shift', 'lock the assignment', 'confirm Yasmines Frühschicht', 'bestätige diese Schicht'. Wraps ConfirmWorkCommand; period-hour recalc runs automatically.

parameters.name: workId
parameters.description: UUID of the Work entry to confirm.

skills.name: unconfirm_work
skills.description: Drops a Work entry from LockLevel=Confirmed back to None so Wizards 1+2+3 can modify the cell again. Cannot drop higher levels (Approved/Closed). Wraps UnconfirmWorkCommand.

parameters.name: workId
parameters.description: UUID of the Work entry to unconfirm.

skills.name: add_schedule_command
skills.description: Places a key command (FREE/-FREE/EARLY/-EARLY/LATE/-LATE/NIGHT/-NIGHT) on a client/date. These are seeds the wizards must respect — e.g. EARLY = only an early shift may be assigned that day, FREE = the agent must stay free. Use when the user says: 'Yasmine am 15.05. frei', 'Coline muss am 11.05. Früh arbeiten', 'set free day', 'lock early'.

parameters.name: analyseToken
parameters.description: Optional scenario UUID; null = main schedule.

skills.name: list_scenarios
skills.description: Lists AnalyseScenarios, optionally filtered by group. Use after start_autowizard finishes to show the user what's available to accept or reject. Returns each scenario's id, name, token, group, and create time.

parameters.name: groupId
parameters.description: Optional group UUID filter. If omitted, returns scenarios across all groups.

skills.name: approve_day
skills.description: Approves all Work entries on a given day for a given group — sets LockLevel=Approved (2). Stronger than confirm: even Wizards on individual cells stop. Returns the number of affected works. Use when the user says: 'tag freigeben', 'approve Monday', 'freigeben am 15.05.'. Wraps ApproveDayCommand.

parameters.name: groupId
parameters.description: Group UUID whose clients' works on that date are approved.

skills.name: revoke_day_approval
skills.description: Drops day approval back to Confirmed — undoes approve_day. Returns the number of affected works. Wraps RevokeDayApprovalCommand.

skills.name: close_period
skills.description: Closes a payment period — sets LockLevel=Closed (3) across all Work entries in the range. Nothing can be edited afterwards (not even confirmed/approved). Returns the number of sealed works. Use when the user says: 'Periode abschliessen', 'schliesse Mai ab', 'close May 2026'. Wraps ClosePeriodCommand.

skills.name: reopen_period
skills.description: Re-opens a closed payment period — drops Closed back to the prior level so works become editable. Returns the number of affected works. Wraps ReopenPeriodCommand.

skills.name: delete_break
skills.description: Soft-deletes a single Break entry (absence: vacation/sick/training). Period-hour recalc runs automatically. Inverse of add_break for rollback purposes.

parameters.name: breakId
parameters.description: UUID of the Break entry to delete.

skills.name: verify_my_last_action
skills.description: Meta-skill: reports the most recent skill execution for the current user in the last 30 minutes. Returns the skill name, success/failure, duration, error message and parameters. Use after a mutating skill call to confirm the action took effect, or before rollback_my_last_change. Does not mutate any data.

skills.name: rollback_my_last_change
skills.description: Meta-skill: proposes an inverse-skill call to undo the most recent SUCCESSFUL mutating execution for the current user. Returns the inverse skill name + parameter hint. Does NOT execute the rollback - the LLM/user must explicitly call the inverse skill after confirmation (HITL contract). Skills without an automatic inverse (e.g. create_employee, accept_scenario) return __manual__ and require human intervention.

skills.name: list_open_wizard_jobs
skills.description: Polls each wizard registry (AutoWizard / Wizard 1 / Wizard 2 / Wizard 3) for a given jobId and returns whether it is still running. Used after start_autowizard or start_wizardN to check status without waiting for the SignalR hub event. Registries are in-memory ConcurrentDictionaries without an enumeration API; pass a concrete jobId.

parameters.name: jobId
parameters.description: Optional UUID. If omitted returns a hint that enumeration is unsupported.

skills.name: add_break
skills.description: Places a single absence (Break) on a client/date - vacation, sick day, training, parental leave. Wraps BulkAddBreaksCommand so the Break macro service computes work-time and period-hour recalc runs. Break hours count toward TargetHours but NOT toward MaxWeeklyHours; the cell becomes immutable for Wizards 1+2+3. Use list_absence_types first to find the right absenceId.

parameters.name: analyseToken
parameters.description: Optional scenario UUID; null = main schedule.

skills.name: add_workchange
skills.description: Adds a WorkChange (correction / replacement / travel / briefing) to an existing Work entry. Wraps PostCommand<WorkChangeResource> so SignalR + period-hour recalc fire. WorkChangeType enum: CorrectionStart / CorrectionEnd / ReplacementStart / ReplacementEnd / ReplacementWithin (need replaceClientId) / TravelStart / TravelEnd / TravelWithin / Briefing / Debriefing.

parameters.name: replaceClientId
parameters.description: Required for Replacement* types - the client taking over.

skills.name: start_wizard1
skills.description: Starts only Wizard 1 (TokenEvolution Planner / coverage-first GA). Use when an initial plan is needed without the smoothing of Wizard 2 and without the LLM Holistic Harmonizer. Returns jobId; completion arrives via the wizard SignalR hub. Auto-resolves agents and shifts from the group when not supplied.

parameters.name: analyseToken
parameters.description: Optional source scenario token; null = main schedule.

skills.name: start_wizard2
skills.description: Starts only Wizard 2 (Harmonizer - Fuzzy/Conductor). Smooths an existing plan without touching coverage decisions and without invoking the LLM. Typically called with the analyseToken of a freshly applied Wizard 1 scenario.

parameters.name: analyseToken
parameters.description: Optional source scenario token to harmonize.

skills.name: start_wizard3
skills.description: Starts only Wizard 3 (Holistic Harmonizer - LLM Vision-Bitmap). Polishes a harmonized plan with multi-agent LLM voting. Consumes LLM credits; use sparingly. Pass the analyseToken of the Wizard 2 scenario.

parameters.name: language
parameters.description: Optional UI language for the LLM stage (e.g. 'de', 'en').

skills.name: cancel_wizard_job
skills.description: Cancels a running wizard job by id. Tries Wizard 1, Wizard 2, Wizard 3 and the AutoWizard orchestrator until one acknowledges. Use when a user says: 'stoppe den Plan', 'cancel wizard', 'abbrechen'.

parameters.name: jobId
parameters.description: UUID of the job to cancel.

skills.name: reject_scenario
skills.description: Rejects an AnalyseScenario — discards its proposed works. Use when AutoWizard output is unacceptable or when a newer scenario supersedes it. Soft-deletes the scenario and its scenario-bound work entries.

skills.name: update_client
skills.description: ALWAYS use this skill to ADD or CHANGE a client's phone, email or address, or to edit master data (name, birthdate, gender, company, title) of an existing employee/customer. Identify the client by firstName+lastName (no search and no id needed — pass the name directly). Supplying street/zip/city ADDS a new address; supplying email or phone ADDS a new communication. Do NOT navigate and do NOT ask the user to edit the form manually — perform the change yourself by calling this skill. Only the supplied fields change.

parameters.name: clientId
parameters.description: UUID of the client to update. Optional if firstName+lastName are given.

parameters.name: street
parameters.description: Adds a new address: street and house number

skills.name: delete_client
skills.description: Soft-deletes a client (employee, customer or extern employee) by id. Soft-delete sets IsDeleted=true; historical works and contracts remain intact and audit-visible. Use for offboarding employees or removing customers from the active list.

parameters.name: clientId
parameters.description: UUID of the client/employee/customer to soft-delete

skills.name: create_group
skills.description: Creates a new group (location, branch, organisational unit) with optional parent for the nested-set hierarchy. ValidFrom defaults to today if not given. Use for opening a new branch or splitting a team.

parameters.name: parentId
parameters.description: UUID of the parent group (omit for root level)

parameters.name: validFrom
parameters.description: ISO date the group becomes active

parameters.name: validUntil
parameters.description: ISO date the group becomes inactive

skills.name: update_group
skills.description: Updates an existing group's name, description and validity dates. Reparenting (moving the group under a different parent) is NOT handled here — use a dedicated move skill so the nested-set invariants stay intact.

parameters.name: groupId
parameters.description: UUID of the group to update

skills.name: delete_group
skills.description: Soft-deletes a group by id. Refuses if the group still has children, unless forceCascade=true is passed; then the whole subtree is soft-deleted.

parameters.name: groupId
parameters.description: UUID of the group to delete

parameters.name: forceCascade
parameters.description: If true, soft-deletes the whole subtree

skills.name: list_groups_hierarchical
skills.description: Returns the group tree starting from rootId (or all roots if omitted) with parent name and depth-level per row. Lets the LLM reason about hierarchy (parent / children / depth) instead of a flat list.

parameters.name: rootId
parameters.description: UUID of the subtree root (omit for all roots)

parameters.name: maxDepth
parameters.description: Max depth from root (1-based); unlimited if omitted

skills.name: create_absence
skills.description: Creates a new absence TYPE (stammdaten — e.g. Sick Leave, Vacation, Education). Concrete employee absences are placed via add_break which references the type by id.

parameters.name: abbreviationDe
parameters.description: Short code (1-6 chars) shown in the schedule grid

skills.name: update_absence
skills.description: Updates an existing absence type (stammdaten). Only supplied fields change. Use to rename, recolor, toggle weekend/holiday inclusion, or switch the paid flag.

parameters.name: absenceId
parameters.description: UUID of the absence type to update

skills.name: delete_absence
skills.description: Soft-deletes an absence type. Refuses if the type is in use by active breaks or break-placeholders, or if marked as Undeletable. Use list_absence_types to inspect usage before calling.

parameters.name: absenceId
parameters.description: UUID of the absence type to delete

skills.name: list_holidays_for_period
skills.description: Returns all holidays falling between fromDate and untilDate for a given country (and optionally a state/canton). Computes holiday dates from CalendarRules via HolidaysListCalculator across all covered years.

parameters.name: state
parameters.description: State / canton abbreviation (BE, ZH, BY, ...). Empty = nationwide only.

skills.name: validate_holiday_overlap
skills.description: Checks whether a specific date is a holiday in the given country + state. Returns IsHoliday=true with the holiday name when it matches, otherwise false. Used before placing a Work/Break to warn about overlap.

skills.name: list_report_templates
skills.description: Lists configured report templates. Optional filter by type: Schedule, Client, Invoice, Absence. Use before generate_period_summary or email_schedule_to_client to pick the right template.

skills.name: generate_period_summary
skills.description: Generates a compact textual summary for a group + period (employee count, day count, employee ids). Klacksy uses this for quick answers like 'how many people in Bern in May' and as a precursor to email_schedule_to_client.

skills.name: read_schedule_state
skills.description: Reads the actual schedule grid of a group + period: which employee is assigned to which shift on which day, including lock level and group-restriction flags. Use this to SEE the current plan before answering questions about assignments, gaps or before proposing changes. Optional analyseToken reads an isolated scenario instead of the real plan. Returns the assigned entries (not the empty slots).

parameters.name: groupId
parameters.description: UUID of the group / planning blade

parameters.name: analyseToken
parameters.description: Optional UUID of a scenario; when set the isolated scenario grid is read instead of the real plan

skills.name: detect_conflicts
skills.description: Detects schedule rule violations for a group + period: rest-time, daily and weekly overtime, consecutive days, minimum rest days per week, and collisions. Use this to JUDGE whether a plan is rule-compliant before confirming or proposing changes. Each conflict carries a severity (error/warning), a stable code and the affected employee + date. Optional analyseToken checks an isolated scenario instead of the real plan.

parameters.name: groupId
parameters.description: UUID of the group / planning blade

parameters.name: analyseToken
parameters.description: Optional UUID of a scenario; when set the isolated scenario is validated instead of the real plan

skills.name: interpret_resource_monitor
skills.description: Reads and interprets the resource monitor (capacity vs. demand over a year) plus current-month shift coverage: where a group is over- or understaffed, which months are critical, and whether covering an absence is worth it. Aggregate capacity-over-time view, complementary to read_schedule_state's grid detail. Returns per-month understaffed/tight day counts, critical months and coverage ratios. Note: the coverage figures are always for the current month regardless of the requested year.

parameters.name: groupId
parameters.description: Optional UUID of a group to focus on; omit for all employees

skills.name: list_scheduling_rules
skills.description: Lists the configured scheduling rule sets and their caps (max work days, min rest days, min pause hours, max daily/weekly hours, max consecutive days, hours targets). Use this to know which rule sets exist and what limits a plan must respect before reasoning about or explaining them. Read-only, no parameters.

skills.name: get_scheduling_defaults
skills.description: Returns the built-in scheduling default fallback limits: min rest hours, max daily hours, max weekly hours, max consecutive days, min rest days per week. These are the hard limits a rule-compliant plan must respect when no contract/rule overrides them. Use this to state or explain the planning limits. Read-only, no parameters.

skills.name: find_replacement
skills.description: Proposes rule-compliant replacement employees for a shift on a day, ranked best-first. Candidates are the group members; one is excluded only when assigning them would cause a collision or a rest-time violation, or when the shift is blacklisted for them. Overtime / consecutive-day / min-rest findings lower a candidate's rank (less headroom) instead of excluding. Preferred employees rank first. Use this to find who can cover an open or to-be-replaced slot. Does not yet check qualifications (v2). Optional analyseToken checks against a scenario.

parameters.name: shiftId
parameters.description: UUID of the shift to fill

parameters.name: groupId
parameters.description: UUID of the group whose members are the candidate pool

parameters.name: analyseToken
parameters.description: Optional UUID of a scenario; when set candidates are checked against the isolated scenario

skills.name: email_schedule_to_client
skills.description: Opens the schedule 'Send to client' dialog for the chosen client and period. The actual PDF generation happens client-side, so this skill returns a navigate UiAction; the user confirms the send in the UI dialog.

parameters.name: clientId
parameters.description: UUID of the client whose schedule should be emailed

skills.name: import_calendar_rules
skills.description: Bulk-imports CalendarRules (holidays definitions) for a country + state. Each entry in rulesJson becomes one rule. Validates every rule via HolidaysListCalculator before persisting; rejects the whole batch on a single bad rule.

parameters.name: country
parameters.description: ISO country code attached to every imported rule

parameters.name: rulesJson
parameters.description: JSON array of { rule, subRule?, nameDe, nameEn?, isMandatory?, isPaid? }

skills.name: add_client_phone
skills.description: Adds a phone number to an existing employee/customer, found by name. Use this directly for 'add/change phone number for <person>' — do not navigate or ask the user to fill a form.

skills.name: add_client_email
skills.description: Adds an email address to an existing employee/customer, found by name. Use directly for 'add/change email for <person>' — do not navigate or ask the user to fill a form.

skills.name: add_client_note
skills.description: Adds a note/annotation to an existing employee/customer, found by name. Use directly for 'add a note to <person>'.

skills.name: add_client_to_group_by_name
skills.description: Adds an existing employee/customer (by name) to a group (by name). Use directly for 'add <person> to group <X>'.

skills.name: remove_client_from_group
skills.description: Removes an existing employee/customer (by name) from a group (by name). Use directly for 'remove <person> from group <X>'.

skills.name: update_client_birthdate
skills.description: Updates the birthdate of an existing employee/customer identified by name. Use this directly for requests like 'change birthdate of <person> to <date>' — do not navigate or ask the user to fill a form.

skills.name: update_client_gender
skills.description: Updates the gender of an existing employee/customer identified by name. Valid values: female, male, legalEntity, intersexuality. Use this directly for requests like 'set gender of <person> to <value>' — do not navigate or ask the user to fill a form.

parameters.name: gender
parameters.description: Gender value: female, male, legalEntity, or intersexuality

skills.name: assign_contract_by_name
skills.description: Assigns a contract to a client/employee — both identified by name. Use this for chat-driven contract assignments where only names are known. Sets the new contract as active and deactivates any previous active contract.

parameters.name: contractName
parameters.description: Name (or partial name) of the contract to assign

parameters.name: untilDate
parameters.description: Optional contract end date (YYYY-MM-DD); leave empty for indefinite
